title: Voting on the environment: Price or ideology? Evidence from Swiss referendums

tax_mentions.context: Solar initiative proposed a 25-year tax on fossil and nuclear energy (CHF 0.001–0.005 per kWh) to fund solar energy and energy efficiency; referendum votes analyzed.
tax_mentions.notes: Tax on fossil and nuclear energy; revenues earmarked for solar promotion and energy-efficiency solutions.

tax_mentions.context: Energy conservation package proposed CHF 0.003 per kWh tax on non-renewable energy for 10–15 years; revenues split across renewables, efficient solutions, hydro maintenance, and programs; votes analyzed.
tax_mentions.notes: Non-renewable energy tax for 10–15 years; revenues allocated to renewables, efficiency, hydro maintenance, programs.

tax_mentions.context: Green tax reform proposed increasing tax up to CHF 0.02 per kWh on non-renewable energy; expected revenues used to reduce social security contributions for employers and employees; referendum support analyzed.
tax_mentions.notes: Revenue recycled by lowering employers’ and employees’ social security contributions.

inclusion_reason: Empirically studies Swiss referendum acceptance on three proposals imposing taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy (solar initiative, energy conservation package, green tax reform), and models determinants of municipal approval rates.
